title: JSON Lines, CSV, HTML Reports, Accessibility, and Correction Workflows · Type Forensics
summary: A preserved TypeScript crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, Fetch, stream, Cheerio, selector, frontier, Promise, browser, axe, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in versioned accessible site-quality report suite and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, an…
objectives: Version JSON Lines records, encoding, ordering, missingness, provenance, error rows, and digest manifest for streaming consumers.
Define headers, delimiters, newline, quoting, Unicode, formula defense, nulls, row limits, and round-trip fixtures with csv-stringify.
Provide landmarks, headings, summaries, sortable-table alternatives, keyboard controls, focus, reflow, status, print, and non-color severity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Correction Workflow

content.text: A debug TypeScript team handles case 4005 during an isolated browser, package, and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove JSONL and CSV contracts, accessible HTML, chart alternatives, corrections, and deterministic evidence; owner authorization and revocation must remain separate from robots and legal-review claims; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Link findings to evidence, owner, status, decision, suppression expiry, retest, changed version, and stakeholder-readable resolution.

options.text: Another successful Correction Workflow run of the same versioned accessible site-quality report suite happy path. That claim fails the correction workflow · fault isolation evidence boundary for Correction Workflow.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Correction Workflow
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-correction-workflow-9j1plg" that uses case 3958 to prove JSONL and CSV contracts, accessible HTML, chart alternatives, corrections, and deterministic evidence. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.
